title: Open dataset + ReleaseManifest + reproducibility package + compute/energy disclosure (CC0)
context: bigger-pi is an open, distributed, verification-first effort to compute more digits of pi on donated compute; the public good is the open, reproducible, independently-verified toolchain, chunk-coordination protocol, and provenance ledger. The release publishes the verified digits as CC0 alongside a ReleaseManifest, a one-command reproduction package, and a compute/energy disclosure; an independent party must be able to re-derive the same finalResultHash, and no secrets or PII may appear anywhere. It depends on run-017.
objective: Publish the verified digits (CC0) with a ReleaseManifest, a one-command reproducibility package, and a compute/energy disclosure that an independent party can reproduce.
acceptanceCriteria: Publishes the digits (CC0), the ReleaseManifest (finalResultHash, two-method agreement, spot-check counts, repro command, compute CPU-hours + energy estimate), and a one-command reproduction package; an independent party re-derives the same finalResultHash.
No secrets/PII anywhere in the release; all artifacts content-addressed.
output: A CC0 dataset release with ReleaseManifest, reproducibility package, and compute/energy disclosure.
